frictions.description: agent-browser tool has broken dependency on playwright-core - module resolution fails with 'Cannot find package' error
frictions.resolution: Attempted 'agent-browser install' but installation timed out after 60 seconds downloading Chromium

frictions.description: FastAPI server won't start on port 8000 - tried multiple launch methods (python main.py, python -m uvicorn main:app) with background processes, but netstat shows no listener on port 8000
frictions.resolution: Attempted multiple server start commands with fireAndForget=true, waited 5-8 seconds between attempts, but curl consistently returns 'Connection refused'

blockers.description: agent-browser tool dependency broken: Cannot find package 'C:\Users\15849\.factory\tools\agent-browser\dist\node_modules\playwright-core\index.js' imported from browser.js
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Ran 'agent-browser install' to download Chromium, but installation timed out after 60 seconds (downloading 172.8 MB)

blockers.description: FastAPI server not starting on port 8000 - multiple launch attempts failed, server process starts but doesn't bind to port. Background processes (PID 70684, 27092, 54300) were started but netstat shows no listener on :8000. Possible causes: missing dependencies, configuration error, or port conflict.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Tried 3 different launch methods: (1) python main.py, (2) python main.py --port 8000, (3) python -m uvicorn main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8000. All started background processes but none bound to port 8000.

summary: Testing blocked: Cannot test either assertion due to infrastructure issues. The agent-browser tool has a broken playwright-core dependency that prevents browser automation. Additionally, the FastAPI server won't start on port 8000 despite multiple launch attempts. Both assertions (m3-a7 and x-a1) require a running server and working browser automation, so no testing could be performed. Recommend: (1) Fix agent-browser playwright-core module resolution, (2) Investigate why server won't bind to port 8000 (check for missing Python dependencies, .env configuration issues, or port conflicts).
